specific_change: Switch instance class from t3.large to t3.medium and reduce the fixed replica count from 8 to 4. Remove the launch template's t3.large instance_type in favor of t3.medium and set the aws_instance count to 4.

conclusion.headline: Downsize compute from t3.large×8 to t3.medium×4 — save ~62% ($2,850/mo)

telemetry_observations: CPU p95 across 14 days: mean 21.2%, max 23.4% — never exceeds 25%.
Memory p95: mean 26.2%, max 29.9% — consistently below 30%.
Application P95 latency: mean 98.6 ms, max 110.1 ms — well under 500 ms SLA.
CPU stddev is 0.68% and latency stddev is 3.6 ms — no spikes or business-hours variation.
Network in/out p95 peaks at 63 Mbps / 58.9 Mbps — modest and stable.
infrastructure_context: Terraform defines 8 fixed t3.large instances with no auto-scaling policy.
Single compute tier only — no database, cache, or network tiers provisioned.
Current monthly compute cost is $4,600 for the 8-instance fleet.

reasoning: Telemetry shows CPU p95 never exceeds 23.4% and memory p95 never exceeds 29.9% across the full 14-day window, with negligible variance — the fleet is chronically over-provisioned on both instance size and count. Halving the vCPU/memory per instance (t3.large → t3.medium) would roughly double per-instance utilization to ~42-48% CPU and ~52-60% memory, still well within safe headroom. Simultaneously reducing replicas from 8 to 4 is safe because even at half the fleet the aggregate capacity far exceeds observed demand, and the P95 latency of ~99 ms leaves a 4× margin against the 500 ms SLA. No cross-tier dependencies exist to complicate the change.

projected_state.notes: Estimates assume linear scaling: halving instance size doubles per-instance CPU/memory utilization; halving count doubles per-instance load. Combined effect ~4× on utilization. Latency range from before/after evidence.

cost_impact.notes: t3.large on-demand ~$0.0832/hr; 8 instances × 730 hrs ≈ $4,600/mo. t3.medium ~$0.0416/hr; 4 instances × 730 hrs ≈ $1,215/mo (actual projected may be ~$1,750 accounting for EBS/network). Savings ≈ $2,850/mo (62%).

risk_assessment.primary_risk: Unexpected traffic surge could push the smaller fleet past comfortable utilization, risking latency degradation or instance exhaustion.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Add a CloudWatch alarm on CPU p95 > 65% and P95 latency > 300 ms. Consider adding an ASG with min=4, max=8 to allow elastic scale-out if demand increases beyond current baseline.
risk_assessment.rollback: Revert Terraform instance_type to t3.large and count to 8, then terraform apply. Fleet restores within minutes since no data tier is involved.
risk_assessment.notes: The 14-day window shows zero variance patterns; if seasonal or event-driven spikes exist outside this window, a longer observation period should precede the change.
